title: Relabel a field and add help text (SAFE)
task_prompt: Change the label of delivery.note.ref from 'Reference' to 'Order Reference' and add a short help tooltip.
failure_mode: NONE — this change is purely cosmetic (a `string=` label and a `help=` tooltip). No schema, security, data, or runtime behaviour changes. It is in the suite as a FALSE-POSITIVE probe: a healthy gate PASSES it. If a result marks safe_task_blocked=true, the gate cried wolf, which erodes trust and trains developers to bypass it. The 'broken_patch' here is a misnomer — it is the safe change under test.

expected_detection.check: NONE — gate MUST pass. Any block on this task is a false positive (record it as safe_task_blocked=true).

notes: Nothing to fix — fixed_patch equals broken_patch on purpose. The metric of interest is per_mode.false_positives; a suite made only of traps would train the gate (and its users) to over-block, so at least one obviously-safe task must ship.

context_files.models/delivery_note.py:
from odoo import fields, models


class DeliveryNote(models.Model):
    _name = 'delivery.note'
    _description = 'Delivery Note'

    ref = fields.Char(string='Reference')
    note = fields.Text()

broken_patch:
--- a/models/delivery_note.py
+++ b/models/delivery_note.py
@@ -5,5 +5,6 @@
     _name = 'delivery.note'
     _description = 'Delivery Note'
 
-    ref = fields.Char(string='Reference')
+    ref = fields.Char(string='Order Reference',
+                      help='Reference shown to the customer.')
     note = fields.Text()
fixed_patch:
--- a/models/delivery_note.py
+++ b/models/delivery_note.py
@@ -5,5 +5,6 @@
     _name = 'delivery.note'
     _description = 'Delivery Note'
 
-    ref = fields.Char(string='Reference')
+    ref = fields.Char(string='Order Reference',
+                      help='Reference shown to the customer.')
     note = fields.Text()
